full_text_pages.text:
Astrology @ The Speed of Light
Author: Kapiel Raaj
© Copyrighted 2011
www.AstrologyKRS.com
All the information in this book should be taken as is,
and must be researched by the reader before implying the
rules of astrology in their own lives. There are many
methods of predicting through astrological charts. I am only
discussing the methods which I found to be helpful and
truthful, but my truth should not be taken as the law.
1

About Me
Who am I and why I wrote this book?
In bullet points…
 Author
 Actor
 Filmmaker
 Entertainer
Even though you hear this in the beginning of every
book on astrology where an astrologer talks about his or her
glorious achievements and predictions, I am going to tell
you the opposite. I am not an astrologer, and I do not have
any awards. What I do have under my belt is over 10 years
of hardcore research on the subject of mysticism, astrology
and numerology.
4

I was like you at one point, blindly following a system
that never made sense, but I still followed like a sheep
following the herd. I tried to make sense out of my weekly
and yearly Sun sign horoscope on websites, but, at the end,
none came true.
I was always around Vedic astrology all throughout my
life since my uncle in India has been practicing Hindu
astrology for 35 years now, but I never really cared for it or
wanted to learn it because it just wasn't my time.
Then, in 2001, I showed my horoscope to an ‘eastern
Vedic astrologer,’ as my mother always told me to do so. He
asked me all the details that I hadn’t been asked before
regarding an astrology chart. He asked my full DOB, place
of birth and time of birth. He then proceeded to ask my
name spelling. This was odd.
That man revealed to me the things I couldn’t have
imagined; that, too, a stranger telling me about my past. He
was looking at weird charts and numbers which didn’t
make much sense to me back then. He was talking about
houses, degrees, signs, and I was like, ‘okay…just tell me
what does my future holds?’ Little did I realize, knowing
those houses and degrees back then, would have made my
prediction a whole lot easier for me to understand, which
also would have made me ask the ‘right’ questions.
After that meeting, I went into the depths of this
art/science. I said science because even though many
Eastern and Greek astrologers still can’t prove this as a
science, there is one method of astrology that can: Jaimini
5

Astrology. Jaimini astrology is one of the main branches of
Jyotish that were forgotten long ago until K.N. Rao in India
revived this method, which to this day has about 90-95%
accuracy in perdition. I personally have not worked with
Jaimini, however I am in the process of learning this method
in the coming years.
In Jaimini astrology, signs, not planets have aspect. For
example, cardinal signs will aspect all the fixed signs except
the one next to it. Fixed signs aspect all cardinal signs,
except the one previous to it. And finally, all the mutable
signs aspect the other mutable signs: Gemini, Virgo,
Sagittarius and Pisces.
There are many layers to this methods, not just sign
aspect but something known as 'Karakas', 'Chara dasha' and
'Karakansha Lagna'.
There are five main branches of astrology in India:
Prashara Hora Shastra
Jaimini Sutra
Bhrigu Nadi Jyotish
KP Astrology
Nadi Astrology
Lal Kitab
What this book discusses is mainly the methods of
Prashara. The one that I found to be complete waste of time
was Lal Kitab. I can write a whole book on as to why it's a
waste of time, but I won't.
6

After knowing all the in-&-out of astrology, I will teach
you the ABCD’s of astrology, and even help construct short
sentences from those ABCD.
This is a secret that not too many blogs, books or people
will tell you. If you are not spiritually ready and do not do
proper prayers, then do not look at your own, or your family
members’ horoscope more than twice a year, but you can
look at anyone other than your family and friends as much
as you like. In India, you will see there are lots of Vedic
astrologers who advise on remedies and miracles, yet they
are giving their services from a broken down house with
hardly any money in their pocket. This is not the question of
why couldn’t they improve themselves first, it’s about, were
they spiritually ready to look at a horoscope? And were they
looking at their own horoscope all day long? If they are not
following the correct rules & regulations, then their lives
will be full of misery. A real astrology who does
consultation professionally will stay away from non-
vegetarian food, smoking, drinking and taking any kind of
drugs; while the astrologer would meditate every morning
as in 'sadhna' to increase his or her intuition.
Another bad habit of mostly all astrologers in India is
that they tell you the bad stuff first before the good stuff and
they say it bluntly. They have no understanding of
psychology or being a psychologist alongside being an
astrologer. Most will tell you up front: "You have a medium
life, you will get divorced twice, you will always struggle for
money, your kids might be mentally retarded and you will
have four big accidents in the next four years." The biggest
problem is that they think they are being so smart and slick
by giving a very fragile person this information. A client
7

comes to a astrologer because they are going through a hard
time, whether it's physical or psychological. They want to be
guided, they want to be healed and they want a friend who
will say everything will be alright. You will hardly find this
type of consultation practice with a American born
astrologer. They know how to tell the bad stuff and they
know how to guide their client through the coming hard
time. This is why I wish there was a legislation in India that
required all Vedic Astrologers to have a degree in
psychology and understanding of human mindset before
trying to give 'spiritual' guidance. It's a shame really how
many get affected by consulting a astrologer, and usually
they never go back and hate this pure divine science.
8

Western Astrology vs. Vedic Astrology
 Greek- 2,000 years old
 Eastern- over 5,500 years old perhaps even
more, but mainly between 5000-12000 years old.
I wish I could just leave this chapter to the bullet points
above, but, I will describe these two historic entities in a
short chapter.
Eastern astrology as in Vedic “Hindu” astrology has
been around in India for past 5.33 thousand years. Some say
the sages of that time knew way more than we do now
about the world, astronomy and science. While others say
those sages back then were visited by beings from other
worlds who taught them the science of planets and
predicting through their movement, like the civilization of
Anunnaki ‘Those who came from heaven’. There are all
kinds of tales and legends regarding the true origins of this
subject. But Vedic astrology goes much more in depth than
just your normal Sun signs, Moon signs and planetary
positions of the west, which I will teach you in this book.
The Greek astrology emerged through the roots of Vedic
astrology, and the reason why we can tell this is because
Greek astrology is missing about 70% of Vedic astrology
predictive methods, including 27 other zodiac signs which
they never acknowledged, along with divisional charts.
9

Eastern astrology doesn’t consider planets such as
Uranus, Neptune, and Pluto in your birth chart, as they are
too far, and take too long to switch zodiac signs in making
any kind of impact on a single individual. The two gas
giants are only used to show the influence they will have on
a whole generation, and, neither are they visible in the sky.
The sages back 5,000 years ago knew that only the planets
that are visible in the sky will impact a person individually.
Those sages were aware of at least three other planets in our
solar system, but did not consider them for astrological use.
The third planet may not have been Pluto, it actually might
have been Nibiru.
I am not bashing the Greek system, but I just do not see
the depth in their astrological system. I had my chart looked
through the Greek system (Western system), and the
predictions were totally wrong. I didn’t say anything to the
Greek/western astrologer, and just listened, but realized
what every Vedic astrologer was talking about. I am using
the term ‘Greek’ and not Western, because our normal Sun
sign based astrology was originated from Greece. Not to
mention, Greek System also got the degrees of the planets
wrong. They went 23 degrees ahead of the Eastern system.
Degrees are points in the sky that determines the length
of each Zodiac sign and the location of the planets in those
signs.
The reason why two systems have different degrees is
because the old Vedic sages determined the position of the
planets from the true rotation of the earth in 24 hour time
period, while the Romans determined the degrees of the
10

planet from the spring equinox point when Sun enters the
first degree of the first zodiac sign Aries. Equinox is known
as the point on the North and South Pole where the earth
wobbles a bit, and shows the planets moving faster from
certain point from earth.
Mayan, Egyptian and Hindu mapping of the sky are
99.999% same, and taken seriously even by the scientific
community around the world.
Remember, George Washington waited for the Moon to
enter into the sign of Virgo the night he was going to lay the
first brick of the White House foundation. This fact will
never be denied even by the highest scholars of our time.
The entire structure of Washington D.C. has been energized
with Black Magic rituals to keep its people, and perhaps the
world under control by its gigantic monuments. Those
structures are believed to be emitting a wave of energy or
radiation which keeps the mind of most people under
control of their agenda. This was the same reason why
Pyramids were built, to energize the minds of its people,
whether it was the Anunnaki or Egyptians who built them.
This practice of magic through structural monuments was
meant for the good by our forefathers of United States, but
later, as the Knights of Malta gained more control on this
side of the hemisphere, things began to change for the worst.
The entire structures in Washington D.C. are based on
astrological symbols.
Most people in today’s world think that Egyptian
pyramids were built 5,000 years ago, including King Khufu’s
face with a body of a lion. This information is highly wrong
11

and demoted by the so called intellects of the PhD board;
because in order to get your degree in archeology in
Egyptian history you have to follow the politically correct
agendas, otherwise say goodbye to your degree. They will
only accept the things they have been taught by the
establishment.
The structures in Egypt are not 5,000 years old as
suggested in high school books. They are over 12-25
thousand years old, maybe even more. The reason why they
are so old because everything in Egyptian culture was
astrologically based. It was known as Astro-theology to
them. This is why, when you study the reason behind the
building of King Kufu’s face, it will not make sense as to
why he was put on a body of a lion? 5,000 years ago, we
were in the age of Scorpio, and there was no reason to build
the structure of King Khufu statue with a body of a lion in
the age of Scorpio; this is because, it was build during the
age of Leo, when the constellation of Leo was rising from the
Eastern Horizon at Khufu’s line-of-sight. The age of Leo was
last present on this planet from 12,000-25,000 B.C. But there
are also theories circulating that it's not the body of a lion at
all, but a body of a dog.
These are the details and truth you will not learn from
any kind of school or astrology book as most of the time
they are subjected to scrutiny and rebuttal. Society only
wants to you follow what the government has laid out and
that is it, while the government holds 80% of the truth about
our planet under cover.
Let's go into something even more controversial:
Christianity.
12

I am not an agnostic, racist or atheist. I am simply
awaken, and this why I am writing this passage.
Through my intensive research, the entire religion of
Christianity is based upon Astro-theology. Jesus Christ, in
the bible is the modification of Egyptian Sun God Amun Ra;
for this is why all Christians say “Amen” at the end of their
prayers. Jesus is the same way. He is the representation of
the Sun; this is why he is called the son of God. The word
sun and son was born from the same ideology. Jesus is the
representation of the planet Sun, and his 12 disciples are the
12 zodiac signs. There are a lot more details in my book
Kyirux II: The Hidden History. My point in this book was to
tell you that everything from religion to powerful
government is based upon the planets and stars. Everything
is astrologically based, but, it is considered a pseudoscience
by the general government intellect and religion figures
because they do not want you to know the real and hidden
secrets to gain wealth and fame.
Saturn, according to most, including the true Vedic
historians, was the main significator of wealth and status, as
it originally controls the 10th and 11th house in the Zodiac
and controls the sign of Capricorn and Aquarius which are
the 10th and 11th zodiac signs. In Hindu culture, Saturn has
been bread to people as the most demonic planet that gives
nothing but sorrow and trouble to people. I wonder why
they say this? Why do some people want to hide the truth
about this well wisher of all beings? Why do they all
promote Venus & Jupiter instead of Saturn as the giver of
wealth?
13

Venus only brings love in the true reality of planetary
energy while Jupiter brings wisdom; it is Saturn and its
energy that brings all that you need and more in life; for this
is why Blue Sapphire is considered the most dangerous
stone in astrology, as it can either kill you, or take you to
new heights, but the fact-of-the-matter is, it only benefits
most of the time, but out of fear people never wear it. It is
supposed to be worn on the neck, or in the middle finger of
either hand. Blue Sapphire captures the invisible rays of the
planet Saturn and transmits it into our nerves as the light
hits our skin from an open back ring or pendant. But
remember, Blue Sapphire and its relationship to Saturn was
never present in Prashara hora shastra. This correlation was
invented in Egypt.
I wonder, if every thought in our brain was also planted
by someone, like movie 'The Matrix.' We think that we as
humans are capable of either destroying or sustaining a
balance on this earth, but, what if our so called brain inside
our skull is manufacturing those thoughts, while in the
conscious reality there is a totally different scenario about
science, mathematics, DNA, genetics and life outside our
own planet. These are the thoughts that began to rattle
within you after you do chakra meditation, which are
energy points in your body that control everything about
you and your DNA. DNA can be separated and
manipulated by sound vibration, which they do now in labs.
This is why mantras chanting changes people, and takes
them to a higher level of consciousness.
When we look at ourselves in the mirror in the morning
we think of ourselves as our house, car, money, academic
arrogance, and ego, but no one stops to think about the fact
14

that, "I am a consciousness in a genetic, bio mechanical suit,
experiencing this dimension and game called life."
The planets, and their radial vibrations, too, are
connected to our chakra energies. By changing your chakra's
energy and you can change your luck; a simple solution to a
humanly made complex system of understanding.
If our DNA can be altered by sound and visual vibration,
don’t you think the government with trillions and trillions of
dollars might not experiment with such technology to keep
their citizens under control through radial and sound
vibration known as music, TV and radio?
They clog your brain with reality shows, cameramen
chasing celebrities, sex, drugs, and treating women like sex
objects in music videos! While all along the educational
system keeps plummeting to its death in United States, and
the plague is soon to follow across the sea. An ex-gang

Basically Earth has two equators, one is called a celestial
equator and the other is called Ecliptic equator. Celestial
equator is the position of the sky as we see from the ground
through our eyes, while the celestial equator is tilted 23
degrees since the entire solar system is titled 23 degrees, so
stars do not just go in a straight circle, the circle is titled 23
degrees from the actual equator. This is what Maha Rishi
Prashara knew back five to twelve thousands years ago.
When someone is born during night time, the Sun is
positioned in Imum Coeli, which is simply the lowest point
for the Sun from the celestial equator. When Sun is on top of
the sky during noon time, it is in the Medium Coeli position,
because like the Ecliptic equator, the Sun goes in a 23 degree
titled circle from the equator. This is why when Sun rises
from the east or simply the eastern side of the sky from
anywhere in the world is always known as the Ascendant or
Rising sign in astrology. This is for both Western & Eastern
system.
Remember, the first house in astrology is always the sign
rising in the east. The terms: Ascendant, rising and the 1st
house of a birth chart is referring to the same thing: the
eastern side of the sky. The west direction where the Sun
sets is called Descendant, or 7th house, as in 7th house of
astrology. North direction is known as the 4th house and
south direction is called the 10th house in astrology. No
matter what time you’re born, whatever sign was rising
from the eastern side of the sky becomes your ascendant or
your rising sign.

Let’s talk about Aspects of Planets
Aspects are simply the houses that each planet looks
towards from the house that it sits in. An aspect simply
means sight, or seeing something. If you’re sitting at a
window of your apartment and you look across the
airshaft to the building across the street; you’re aspecting
that building.
Instead of you, just put Saturn at the window with a
cup of coffee, and instead of the apartment building,
think of a astrological house; the 12th houses for
instance.
Saturn, sitting at the same window that you were
sitting at but now in the 12th house, he will look towards
building number 1 (which is the place you’re sitting at,
inside of the apartment), building number 3, building
number 7 and building number 10. And when he looks at
those buildings, he affects the ambience of those
buildings. Simply, Saturn aspects the 1st, 3rd, 7th and
10th house from itself. This is why in the 12th house,
Saturn would aspect the 2nd house, 6th house and 9th
house. All aspects are counted counterclockwise.
Let's say Saturn is sitting at the window of an
apartment building 1 (The first house, aka, Ascendant),
and he’s looking at his girlfriend in building (house) 3,
but, because he’s inside a bad apartment (building 1),
61

he’s not happy, and so he’s looking at his girlfriend
across the airshaft with a grim and somber face. What
does this do? Well, it will bring the girlfriend's mood
down in building 3, and the ambience of building 3 will
also suffer because the negative energy will transpire
through the girlfriend and into the house. This will
happen to the building 7 and 10 as well (meaning
houses), because Saturn is not just looking at build 3 with
a somber look, but all the buildings he is able to see.
Building 7 and 10 may hold his father and friends, and it
will not be a good feeling.
Now, let's say Saturn was in a good (Happy) position
in building 1, which is the apartment he’s sitting in right
now, then he’ll be happy, and smiling, and giving kisses
to his girlfriend, father, mother, and, his boss,  , which
will just create a happy mood all around.
Happy and sad mood in astrology is simply the signs
and houses a planet sits in where he or she is either
happy being there, sad or neutral.

What is a retrograde planet?
Retrograding (Vakri) simply means the planet is moving
backwards in the sky, but is it really? No… it’s not. It’s just
from our point of view it looks like the planet is moving in
the opposite direction compared to last week. This is called
retrograding, and it does have its own effect on a person’s
chart. If it’s not retrograding its going direct. This occurs due
to the yearly wobble of the earth from its north and South
Pole.
RAHU/KETU- They always retrograde, they never go
direct. In your astrological chart made from software, you
will see Rahu and Ketu as retrograded, or a symbol such as
‘Retro’, this is the case with every single human on this
planet. No one has direct Rahu & Ketu.
Also very important, SUN and MOON never go
retrograde either. Why? Well, just ask yourself, when was
the last time you saw Sun rising from the west and setting in
the east, same for the Moon. The simple answer is NEVER.
They never go retrograde.
Planets that are retrograde, intensify their quality and the
quality of the planet they are sitting with. During the period
of such planet the native would be far more detailed and
prepared compare to other people, but to be honest,
retrograde planets most of the time give the same result as
direct planets. If you have a retrograde planet, first think of
it as being direct, then add a bit more detailing power to it.
Retrograde planets tend to look back and make sure
75

everything is done right. They keep looking back and seeing
if they made any mistake in their previous work.
What is a Conjunction?
Conjunction simply means union of planets. They occur when two
or more planets are in the same house. You have two, three, four,
five, six, seven, even eight planetary conjunctions. If you look at
the chart Feb 4th 1962, all the planets would be in the same house,
especially around 3:00pm time in Santa Monica, Ca. (excluding
Uranus, Neptune and Pluto)
How do conjunctions affect your chart? Conjunction form a
special relationship called yogas in Vedic astrology. Conjunction
of two or more planets forms a distinctive personality & events for
each individual.
How do you study a conjunction?
Conjunction can be tight or loose depending upon how closer or
far apart two planets are in degree. If a single zodiac sign is 30
degrees long, then two planets can be anywhere between 0-
29:59:00 degrees apart, however, a difference of 10 or more
degrees usually lessens the effect of the conjunction.
i.e. I
Mars and Jupiter in conjunction means Mars and Jupiter are in the
same house in your birth chart, but this can have different results.
If Mars is at 5 degrees Libra in the 2nd house along with Jupiter at
8 degrees in Libra, then this is a very close conjunction, where
Mars and Jupiter will influence each other.
76

If Mars is at 5 degrees Libra in the 2nd house along with Jupiter at
18 degrees in Libra, then this is a very loose conjunction, where
Mars and Jupiter will not influence each other that much.
But two planets forming a conjunction will not always be active,
they will only become active during their time periods known as
mahadasha and antar dasha, which you will learn later on in the
book.
Think of tight and loose conjunction as in two roommates living
together in an apartment. A tight conjunction will have both
roommates in the same room, while a loose conjunction will have
them in the separate room or even separate floor.
Since I said union of planets creates yogas as in combination of
different situation in life like wealth, marriage, leadership, misery,
accidents, health, fame and education, the abundance of positive
yoga and devastation of bad yoga depends upon how close two
planets are that are forming such yoga and where they are located
in your chart. You will learn about yogas later in the book.
The way to study a conjunction is to understand the basic
significance of each planet and looking at what area of life they are
ruling in your chart.
If we look at the example of Mars and Jupiter conjunction and we
assume they are in tight conjunction in the 2nd house then the way
we would study this is by first knowing what 2nd house is. Second
house is things we hold as value, as in our beliefs, money, assets,
family, intake of food and what we speak since 2nd house
represents throat. Mars is our will power, energy and were we
perform our duty like a solider. Jupiter is our ability to preach,
teach and the type of things we believe in like religion, philosophy
and education. Now, the way to combine these three things is by
saying that such a person has a lot of energy in their speech and
they firmly believe in their values and family. They are a spiritual
77

warrior who speaks with strong determination. Since this
conjunction is occurring in Libra in my original example, it shows
through their bold speech and determine beliefs, they run into
major ups & downs in life, especially with their siblings, spouse
and co-workers, why? because Libra in the 2nd house means this is
a Virgo ascendant, and for Virgo ascendant Mars rules the 3rd
(siblings) and 8th (sudden events) house while Jupiter rules the 7th
(marriage, partnership, spouse) and 10th (career field) house.
Now depending upon which one is a functional malefic and
functional benefic or which house Mars and Jupiter rule, that will
determine if this conjunction is auspicious or inauspicious. For this
example Mars becomes the functional malefic and Jupiter is the
functional benefic and this conjunction will bring ups & down in
family life.

Dikbala is a sight and placement of a planet which is its

overcome that bad zodiac sign and the degree placement.

LET’S TALK ABOUT NAKSHATRA
(CONSTELLATIONS) in the Sky.
In astronomy there are points in the sky which make the
Aries goat, and the Taurus bull, so-on-&-so-forth.
But, there are 27 additional constellations in the sky
which Greeks forgot to note down when they were copying
the eastern astrology guide.
These 27 constellations are again ruled by planets. In
Jyotish, they are called Nakshatras.
Nakshatra provide a deeper look into our mind, and our
‘future’. The Nakshatras give a detailed analysis on how this
person’s life will be in all aspects. Nakshatras, according to
my studies, show a much better outlook about a person than
your 12 zodiac signs. You may be a Scorpio Sun, or a Gemini
Moon, but your Nakshatra at the time of your birth is what
will give you the 100% accurate personality traits about you.
What determines a Nakshatra?
Nakshatra is found from your Moon sign. The Zodiac
sign your Moon occupies at a certain degree, this will tell the
Nakshatra you are ruled by. However all planets are in
certain nakshatra.

Nakshatra of Mrigashira (Deer's head).

eyes to other houses.

What are Karaka in Vedic Astrology?
In a birth chart, certain planets are responsible for certain things no
matter where they are placed in the horoscope. They are known as
the significators.
i.e.
If you have a cancer ascendant, then 2nd house is controlled by
Leo, whose ruler is the Sun. That means the native's wealth, family
and speech is controlled by the condition of the sun. However,
Jupiter is the karaka or the (significator) of the 2nd house. This
means, even though Sun might be well placed, if Jupiter is not in a
good position in this horoscope, then wealth, family and education
could suffer or will give average result. Whenever you are trying
judge the condition of the 2nd house, don't just look at the lord of
the 2nd house, unless it's Jupiter himself, but also look at the
placement and condition of Jupiter who is the karaka of the 2nd
house.
Below are the planets and the houses they are 'karaka' of:
Sun: The ascendant, 10th house
Moon: 4th house
Mercury: 2nd and 10th house (2nd house because it represents our
speech and speaking ability which is mainly controlled by
Mercury)
Venus: 4th house, 7th house
Mars: 3rd house and 6th house
Jupiter: 2nd house, 5th house, 9th house and 11th house
Saturn: 6th house, 8th house, 10th house, 12th house

What is a Dasha?
(Planetary time periods)
Dasha - it simply means “A given time period”. It is also
known as (Vimshottari dasha).
In Vedic astrology, your life span is control by a single
planet at certain points of your life. Even though your entire
chart is affecting you, the majority of events will be
happening of a planet that is controlling your life for that
time period, and depending upon its placement in your
chart, it will provide either good or bad time in your life.
The Dasha system is determined by your Moon nakshatra.
Below is the chart of time period of a single planet in
dasha system. This applies to everyone, but no one runs the
same time period. If you’re in Saturn time period, then your
wife might be in Venus time period. To determine what time
period you are operating currently, you must look at the
'Nakshatra' your Moon is placed in during your birth. But
the software will automatically will calculate your
mahadasha time period.
Mahadasha = major planetary time period
Antra dasha = sub planetary time period
104

Just like how the sign of Taurus always comes after
Aries, and how Gemini comes after Taurus, the table below
also never changes. So if you were born in the dasha of
Rahu, then once Rahu is complete, you will not start from
Mars, but you will start from Jupiter dasha, since that is the
next on in the cycle.
Let’s say you were born on November, 6th 1985, at 6:00
pm in California. This means, your Moon was placed in the
sign of Leo, in the 6th house. This means, you were in the
Nakshatra of “Magha” ruled by Ketu.
Depending upon the degree and zodiac sign of your
Moon during the time of birth will determine which
“Mahadasha” you’ll start your life in. In our example above
of Moon in Leo, in the Nakshatra of Magha, which is ruled
by Ketu, the baby will start his time period from Ketu,
which is a 7 year time period. Now, depending upon how
105

good or bad Ketu is in baby’s chart, will tell you how good
those first 7 years will be for the baby. But, what will be his
next Dasha period after 7 years? Well, look at the chart
above and see what comes after Ketu; yes, Venus, the baby
will then start his Venus cycle of 20 years after his 7th
birthday. I made the chart in chorological order as followed
in Vedic astrology. Also remember, baby may not have full 7
year cycle, because Moon's degree may only give 4 years of
Ketu cycle because Moon was half way done with Ketu's
naksahtra.
After the baby goes through its Ketu, Venus, Sun and
Moon cycle, does this mean it will die, no, it means he will
go back to top of the chart and start his Mars period of 7
years. A full planetary cycle lasts for 120 years, which most
of us will not live up to, at least not now.
Dasha will bring the situation related to that planet
which controls a certain sign and house it is placed in. Also,
the aspect that planet is showing in your birth chart will also
bring about situation related to those aspect.
Why is Dasha period important, well, it tells you the
timing of certain events in your life, like marriage, career,
education, when you should do this or when you should not
do that, is all depended upon the Dasha. A Vedic astrologer
will look at these Dashas to give you timings of the events.

Mr. Krishnamurti simply took a zodiac sign, which is

clear picture of the planetary effect.

How to judge a dasha?
This process will become an easy task but in the beginning it
will look a bit complicated.
When judging if your dasha will be good or bad you must
take in account of several factors.
The dasha lord itself is the primary factor. The maha dasha
lord whose cycle you're running should be judge on these
factors below.
 exalted sign
 own sign
 friendly sign
 enemy sign
 debilitated
110

 In conjunction with a functional malefic
 In conjunction with an enemy
 Aspects on the dasha lord
 Is it in Kendra or Trikona
 Dasha lord placement in divisional charts like D3, D9
and D10
 What are the points of the planet in 'Dik bal' and
'Shad Bal'
 Depositor of the mahadasha lord
Exalted: Is your dasha lord exalted in a certain sign? like
Jupiter in cancer or Saturn in Libra where a planet gains
maximum strength. This will help the planet in giving the
maximum amount of positive result during its dasha and
antar dasha period.
Own sign: Is the planet placed in its own sign: Saturn in
Capricorn or Aquarius, Jupiter in Sagittarius or Pisces or
Sun in Leo etc... This is perhaps is even a stronger placement
per my studies as a planet feels most comfortable in its own
sign, since it's its own house. Any person will feel
comfortable in their own house rather than in a luxury hotel
of a exalted sign.
Friendly Sign: Is the planet placed in a friendly sign? like
Jupiter in Leo, Aries or Scorpio, or Venus in Aquarius,
Mercury in Libra. This position is not as strong as the two
above but this still gives decent result in dasha period.
Enemy sign: Now if a planet is placed in an enemy sign, it
can give quite a bit of struggle and trouble during its dasha.
If Sun is in Capricorn, Saturn in Leo or Cancer, Mercury in
Aries or Sagittarius. Here, the planet is forced to live in the
111

house of enemy where it's constantly worried and paranoid.
The planet is agitated being in a foreign yet enemy
environment.
Debilitated: If the dasha lord is debilitated then it can give
lot of downward cycle in the horoscope if other factors are
not present. If Sun is in Libra or Saturn is in Aries, then
planet lose their dignity and status, which can make a
person insecure during its dasha, they can lose their
reputation and status in society during their dasha etc.. But
sometimes these planet can even give lot of wealth. If Mars
is debilitated then during its dasha it can give accidents and
surgery but at the same time it will give you a very
aggressive nature to get what you want. You can be forceful
and threaten people in getting your way, like a gangster or
mafia member. You can scam people and make money, but a
certain placement of a debilitated planet can also land you in
jail due to those things, especially if debilitated planet is in
the 12th house.
Functional malefic: another way to see if the dasha will
have trouble is by seeing if daha lord is in conjunction with a
functional malefic, especially less than 5 degrees. I've
discussed functional malefic in this book, where every
ascendant has a planet that becomes a trouble some planet
even though it might be a benefic like for Cancer ascendant,
Jupiter is the lord of the 6th house, which makes him a
temporary malefic planet for this ascendant. It can cause
debts and diseases and lot of obstacles for the native if it's
not well placed in the chart. i.e. if in cancer ascendant Moon
and Jupiter are in conjunction in the 10th house, especially
less than 5 degrees apart, then during moon dasha the
person can inquire debt due to career and business, they can
112

suffer from depression since moon is the mind and Jupiter is
the 6th house lord of diseases and their own guru and
teacher can become an obstacle for them. However, Jupiter
can give good result in its dasha or antar dasah if it's placed
in its own sign in the 6th house, 8th or 12th house as
functional malefic for cancer ascendant, which will create a
Vipreet Raj Yoga, (reversal of fortune), this shows when a
person who is suffering in life will suddenly win a lottery or
suddenly gain a lot of wealth. If Jupiter is exalted for Cancer
ascendant as a functional malefic, then it shows victory of
enemies, strength to over come obstacles and repaying of
debt on time, but even an exalted Jupiter can give health
problems and other things.
Conjunction with enemy: If two planets are not friendly to
each other then during their dasha they can give mixed
results due to their planetary war. If Saturn and Mars are in
conjunction, then during each of their dasha they take the
native to higher management status including fire fighter,
engineer etc.. but, they can also get lot of cuts & bruises, car
accidents and burns due to Mars being fire, Saturn being oil
and mixing them together is not a very healthy choice, not to
mention Saturn and Mars are not too friendly to each other.
Aspects on dasha lord: You must also check if dasha lord or
antar dasha lord is receiving an aspect from a friendly planet
or an enemy planet. If the dasha lord is well placed, but it
has an aspect from a debilitated or enemy planet then it can
cause set backs again. If the dasha lord is reciving an aspect
from a friendly plaent who is strong and well placed then it
will add to the support for the planet during its dasha
period.
113

Kendra & Trikona placement: another way to see if the
dasha will be good or bad is to see if the planet is in Kendra
as in house 1, 4, 7 and 10 from the ascendant or house 1, 5
and 9 from the ascendant. Any planet in Kendra becomes
powerful, but the trikona placement is a bit tricky, if a
malefic planet comes into trikona house then it does cause
some trouble unless that malefic planet is exalted or in its
own sign in those houses. i.e. Saturn in 5th house (trikona)
house for Gemini ascendant is exalted, as 5th house has
Libra rising in it. Mars in the 9th (trikona) house for Taurus
ascendant is exalted in Capricorn; such placement gives
good result vs if Mars was in Gemini or Saturn was in cancer
in the trikona house.
Divisional chart placement: Not only do you check for all
the things I mentioned above in the birth chart, but you
must also check of all those things in the divisional charts,
especially D9 and 10 chart, which is about basic fortune,
marriage life and career. If a strong planet in D1 (your birth
chart) is debilitated or weak in the divisional chart, then the
dasha of that planet or antar dasha of that planet will give
mixed results. Even the D3 chart should be checked which is
about basic happiness and gains through siblings. If a dasha
planet is weak in the D3 chart then their could be general
unhappiness during that time. A planet doesn't have to be
exalted in the divisional chart, but as long as they are in
good sign and position they only add to the strength of the
D1 chart.
(One thing you must be careful of when looking at divisional
charts is the accuracy of birth time, because divisional charts
change every 5 to 15 minutes depending upon the chart. This is
why it's very important to have the correct birth time. If you only
114

'think' you were born around a certain time, where the actual time
could be 15 minutes off, then it's better not to study the divisional
charts. However, there is one thing you can do. You can check the
sign planets of the planets in the divisional chart and judge their
strength based on the sign, even though it will only show half the
story, but you'll have a good idea of the planet's personality since
planets in divisional charts don't change signs that quickly.)
Even though I have spoken about Bahva Chalit Chart and
how they are used in seeing the true result of a dasha,
however, I will not advocate this in the true concept of
looking at the dasha result because it will only confuse you
more as a new student to Jyotish. Only an expert who has
been studying Jyotish for 10 years or more should pay
attention to the bhava chalit chart. Although in K.P. System
they use bhava chalit house placement all throughout, you
can predict just as good with the basics principles that I am
teaching you here. Do not worry about chalit charts, there
are so many chalit charts, with each giving different result,
anyone can be driven to madness.
Whenever you study a divisional chart, the same principle
of aspect, conjunction and yoga apply. If a planet is
receiving a 'Neecha banga raja yoga', in a divisional chart,
then it helps the main birth chart to over come hurdles
during the dasha of that planet. Yogas play a big part also in
judging the strength of the dasha; whether it is a raj yoga,
dhan yoga or neech banga raj yoga, they add littlie bit of
strength and points towards the main birth chart.
Planet Bal: In Vedic astrology there are mathematical
calculation giving points to a planet. One of the basic ones is
Dik Bal, meaning 'directional strength'. Each planet gains a
directional strength in the chart by being in a certain house.
115

Sun in 1st house and 10th house, Jupiter in 1st house and 4th
house, Mercury in 1st house, Saturn in 7th house, Venus and
Moon in 4th house, Mars in 10th house, Rahu in 6th house
and Ketu in 12th house. In this case, if Sun is debilitated in
the 10th house, that means it still has its strength even
though it's weak by sign placement. This shows that even
though this person can suffer a lack of reputation and self-
esteem in career field, they will still manage to prevail in
their professional sphere in midlife because Sun gets its
directional strength in the 10th house.
Shad bal is simply the total calculation of all the above
aspects I mentioned, which are accumulated by seeing the
planet's sign placement, house placement, aspect, time of
day the native was born and degrees. The entire calculation
of this gets certain points in a full horoscope report. One
thing to notice when looking at Shad bal is to also check and
see if you were born on the Moon waning or waxing time
period, or if it was a full or no moon. This part is given
attention especially during Moon maha dasha analaysis.
Antar dasha works the same way, you must look at the sub
lord period and their entire strength in the horoscope. But,
one way to see the placement of sub lord strength is not just
by their position from the ascendant but also from the dasha
lord itself. If you are running through Mercury dasha, then
any planet placed in Kendra or Trikona from Mercury is
considered good, even 3rd house and 11th house from
ascendant or the dasha lord themselves give lots of gain
during their sub period. Dasha lord placed 3rd and 11th
from the ascendant also give good results, especially malefic
planets like Saturn, Mars, Rahu, Ketu and Sun. Antar dasha
116

simply bring situation related to other houses through the
eyes of the house where maha dasha planet is placed.
Just because you go through the dasha of 7th house planet,
doesn't mean you will only experience things related to 7th
house only. If you go through Jupiter dasha of 16 years,
while it's placed in the 7th house, that doesn't mean you will
only deal with marriage, legal partnership and other people;
you will deal with the situation related to Jupiter's
placement, the houses it rules in your horoscope, but you
may also inquire debt in this time, get into accidents, get a
promotion, make a foreign travel trip etc.. this only happens
because other sub lord periods (antar dasha) come into play,
yet through the situation related to the 7th house.
i.e. you might get into debts due to legal partnership, you
might get into accident while traveling with your spouse,
you might go to foreign lands due to your spouse or you
may succeed in business due to partnerships and marriage.
Depositor of mahadasha lord: Depositor simply means the
ruler of the house. If Jupiter is sitting in Taurus, then the
landlord of Jupiter will become Venus. When judging the
time period of Jupiter, you should also look at the placement

mahadasha period of Jupiter it will give weak results.

together at the right place and the right time. The success

Not only our horoscope works towards or against us, but

The best horoscope will become a billionaire in United States

This is why my main purpose of the book is not to tell you if

That's all we need to know right now and after death, is our

To simplify this entire concept of dasha let me give you a
basic principle of finding the result of such time periods.
Whichever planetary time period you're running, that planet
will give result based on it's own significance, the house that
it's placed in and of the house of his or her moolatrikona
sign.
What is Moolatrikona signs?
Most planet in astrology besides Sun and Moon have
lordship over two signs.
Saturn- Capricorn, Aquarius
Mars- Aries, Scorpio
118

Jupiter- Sagittarius, Pisces
Venus- Taurus, Libra
Mercury- Gemini, Virgo
Sun- Leo
Moon- Cancer
The Moolatrikona sign for each planet is:
Saturn- Aquarius
Mars- Aries
Jupiter- Sagittarius
Venus- Libra
Mercury- Virgo (also his exaltation sign)
Sun- Leo
Moon- Taurus/Cancer
Rahu/Ketu have no mooltrikona sign, they give results
based on the house and sign they are placed in.
If you are going through Saturn mahadasha, while Saturn is
placed in your 5th house in the sign of Libra, which means
this is a Gemini ascendant horoscope, then Saturn will give
result of what Saturn itself represents which is discipline,

sitting in the 5th house and what 5th house represents:
creativity, speculative business, romance, love, cinema,
acting and academic studies. Then finally the 9th house
effect where his 'moolatrikona' sign is rising: Aquarius. 9th
house which is higher learning, teachers, long distance
travel, your belief system and feeling fortunate. It will give
light effect of 8th house which is also ruled by Saturn
119

(Capricorn), and don't forget, Saturn will also act like the
planet who controls the nakshatra its sitting in.
Since Saturn is exalted in this example, Saturn will give
good results of all the three principles unless of course it's in

Another rule of judging a dasha is putting the dasha lord as
the ascendant and judging the chart in that particular time
period.
That means if Saturn dasha is running and Saturn is in the

ascendant in Libra ascendant for that time period, and you'll
be able to judge 'antar dasha' effect through it.
Another great way of judging a dasha is looking at the
planetary position from the moon, as in 'Moon rashi chart',

One important thing, before we move on. Each planet in

Let’s go into more depth. A single Nakshatra, for
example, “Rohini” Nakshatra, is divided into 4 different
parts called “PADHA”.
I.e.
Rohini is 10 degrees Taurus to 23 degree Taurus, but in
that total 13 degrees, there is a division of 4.
Rohini PADHA 1 -10 to 13 degrees
Rohini PADHA 2 – 14-16 degrees
Rohini PADHA 3 – 16-19 degrees
Rohini PADHA 4 – 19- 23 degrees.
124

Each “Padha” has its own personality inside a single
Nakshatra. If there were 4 babies born in 4 different Padha
of a single Nakshatra, then those 4 babies will have different
personalities and different outcomes in life. This is why

and Moon only control 1 Nakshatra, while rest control 2 or
more.

Greek has no certain planetary combinations identified

(Dosha stands for a negative combination, and Pitra means
past life Karma.) This is just one example of over 500
combinations. How important are they you would ask? Not
very important, but they help you understand your life path.
Just because you have a (Raj Yoga) in your horoscope
doesn’t mean you will become a king, a president, or a head
of some organization. There are many charts that I studied

Neech Banga Raja Yoga: This is one yoga that should
matter most of the time in astrology. This yoga actually
makes a weak planet into a powerful planet, more powerful
than its own exalted position. I’d rather have a Neech Banga
raja yoga than just an exalted planet.
i.e.
Sun is weak in Libra and exalted in Aries.

is in Aries, Scorpio or Capricorn from the ascendant, then

Sun is exalted in the sign of Aries, and lord of Aries is Mars,
that means, if Mars is sitting in its own sign, or is excelled
from the Ascendant or Moon, then Mars is lifting Sun out
from the gutter and on to a throne.

Sun a bit stronger, but if Venus and Sun were in Kendra
houses then it would be considered a Neech Banga.
i.e. III

aspect each other from 7th position or from an aspect that
mutually makes them look at one & other, this aspect makes
the chart active and alive. When a planet is not looking at
any planet, then that planet becomes blind.

What is a Raj Yoga?
Raj yoga simply means 'Kingly yoga' as in combination
for throne, or sitting on a throne. Back in the days around 5
to 7 thousand years ago there was no electable government,
congress, Prim Minister, voting, and especially void of 98%
of the jobs that are now available. Back then when Maha
Rishi Prashara was writing this genius text as part of the
Vedas, applied the meaning of Raj Yoga to working under
the king or actually becoming the king himself. But due to
the class system there were several types of Raj Yoga, the
highest being 'Maharaj Yoga'.
Since 5,000 years ago, Raj yoga meant something totally
different, it has completely changed its meaning in the
digital age. If you have Raj yoga it doesn't mean you will
become a king, President or Prim Minister of a country.
Having a Raj Yoga means you will become a manager at
your office, supervisor at your company or high level
executive as in Vice-President or distinct manager. If you
have a Raj Yoga yet you have been working at a retail store
for 10 years, then it could mean two things. 1. You can
become the manager of that store or you will go to another
job where you will be promoted to a higher post.
If you look at a more mundane meaning of Raj yoga, it
simply means managing people or taking care of other
people, which is exactly what the King's job was, to take care
171

of his people in the kingdom. As a store manager in 2010
you are simply managing people under you.
But Raj yoga happens when lord of the kendra and lord
of the trikona are in conjunction kendra or trikona.
i.e.
See how Moon and Jupiter or Moon and Sun are in
conjunction as lord of kendra and trikona. This creates a
powerful Raj yoga.
Another example is yogakarka Raj yoga. This is when
one single planet is capable of giving Raj yoga by being lord
of the kendra and trikona.
172

ascendant are the only ones with yogakarka planet.

Leo- Mars
Libra- Saturn

Aquarius Venus
i.e.

Another way a Raj yoga occurs is when kendra or

Do Rahu and Ketu form Raj yoga in a birth chart or
divisional chart? (Yes, they do)
Rahu & Ketu are also capable of giving Raj yoga in a
birth chart. This happens when Rahu & Ketu are in
conjunction with a kendra or trikona lord while sitting in
Kendra or trikona. If Rahu and Ketu are sitting alone in
Kendra or trikona while their lords are in kendra or trikona
makes them Raj yoga giving planets, however the kendra or
trikona lord should not be in 6th, 8th or 12th house, then it
becomes a weak Raj yoga which may not come to fruitarian
or might give very weak results.
If Rahu or Ketu are in kendra and get an aspect from lord
of the kendra or trikona, that too, while the lord of such
houses is placed in kendra or trikona, that too, creates a Raj
yoga by the nodes.
The biggest point to consider for Raj Yoga is the degrees
between the two planets in conjunction. If the degree
distance between two planets is more than 15 degrees then
it's really not a true conjunction. It means two planets are in
the same house but on different floors. If two planets are less
174

than 10 degrees apart then they are on the same floor of the
house and if they are less than 5 degrees apart then they are
in the same room, which makes the communication between
two planets strong. But, remember, if this happens to be Sun
then most planets will become combust if they come less
than 10 degrees from Sun. Only Mercury can be as close as 1
degree apart and still not be combust since Mercury is
always near the Sun and is used to its heat.
If Rahu and Ketu are too close to Sun and Moon then Sun
and Moon become eclipsed which means only after midlife
will these shadow planets will remove their dark cloud.
Many people in the world have Raj yoga but they are
struggling from pay check to pay check, why is that? Raj
yoga doesn't equal to dhan yoga. You can be a manager at a
restaurant, retain store or your local city office, but does that
mean you're rich? That question is answered by 'Dhan yoga'.
What is a Dhana yoga?
Dhana yoga means 'wealth giving yoga'.
This is perhaps the most important yoga everyone looks
for in their horoscope. Wealth is God in Kalyug and
everyone seeks God, or most of us do.
The strongest dhan yoga that is formed in a horoscope
occurs between 2nd, and 11th house lords, where 11th being
the strongest. The other houses that are involved are 5th and
9th house.
175

A planetary relationship between 2nd and 11th houses
occurring in kendra or trikona creates a powerful wealth
giving yoga. But this combination must occur in kendra,
trikona or 2nd and 11th house only.
What causes dhan yoga?
 if 2nd or 11th house lord are in 2nd or 11th house.
 2nd house lord in 11th house, 11th house lord in 2nd
house creating a (parivartana yoga)
 Any parivartana yoga occuring between 2nd, 5th, 9th
and 11th house is also dhan yoga.
 2nd house lord in 5th house while 5th house lord in
2nd house.
 11th house lord in 5th house while 5th house lord is
in 2nd, 11th or 9th house.
 conjunction between 2nd and 11th house lord in
kendra or trikona.
 conjunction between 9th house lord with 2nd and
11th in kendra or trikona.
 2nd house lord in conjunction with trikona house
sitting in trikona or kendra.
 11th house lord in conjunction with kendra or trikona
lord.
 These combination should not be in their debilitation
or weak sign otherwise dhan yoga is weakened.
 Rahu and Ketu placed in 2nd and 11th house also
give powerful dhan yoga especially if they in
conjunction with the 2nd or 11th house lord.
 2nd, 5th, 9th or 11th lord aspecting each other while
they in kendra, trikona or 2nd and 11th house creates
a dhan yoga.
176

The most important thing to remember about dhan yoga
is that 11th house becomes the most important, and any
planet influencing the 11th house must be judged to
determine the strength of wealth.
If an exalted planet aspects the 11th house, sits in the
11th house or is in conjunction with the 11th house lord in
kendra, trikona, 2nd or 11th house, then it will give wealth
during its maha and antar dasha.
11th house is the house of incoming gains, while 2nd
house is the house of savings or your accumulated wealth. If
the 2nd house is strong but the 11th house is weak, then
what's the point of having the ability to save money if you
don't have any incoming gains? But, everyone gets a
paycheck, and people with good 2nd house are able to save
more than their weather counterparts sometimes.
If your 11th house is good but your 2nd house is
damaged, then all the incoming gains will be flushed out
and it will be hard for you to save money.
5th and 9th house are basically the houses that give us
happiness. They are not really the wealth giving houses. The
reason why 5th house is called the house of lakshmi and 9th
house is called the house of fortune because 5th house is the
house of speculative career like stock market, cinema,
writing, lottery, betting which can sudden give huge amount
of wealth. 9th house shows how fortunate we feel in life.
People can be rich but they feel very unfortunate in life. On
the flip side you can have someone making minimum wages
but they might feel very fortunate in life to be even making
177

the money they are making. Someone with a good 9th house
will not complain about having lack of money, they will see
the glass as half full than half empty.
Sudden wealth is also seen through vipreet Raj Yoga
(reversal of bad fortune) which occurs when lord of 6th, 8th
and 12th house is sitting in 6th 8th or 12th house.
Ascendant lord also is an important factor of receiving
dhan yoga. If ascendant lord is in conjunction with 2nd and
11th lord while sitting in 2nd, 11th, Kendra or Trikona, then
in its mahadasha and antar dasha the native will receive
wealth, but make sure that these planets are not debilitated,
or in enemy sign. If Ascendant lord is in conjunction with
5th or 9th house, that too makes a powerful dhan yoga.
i.e.
For Leo ascendant if Moon (lord of the 12th house) is
sitting in the 6th house then it creates a vipreet Raj yoga. The
native will sudden gain lot of wealth in their life. This can be
one of those rag or riches stories or homeless man winning a
million dollar lottery.
The timing of such wealth giving yoga is always seen
through dasha system. All the wealthy people around the
world made their wealth during maha and antar dasha of
dhan yoga giving planets but once the yoga is activated it
might keep giving wealth even after the dasha has finished,
but you need the ignition from the dhan yoga engine to
ignite that fire in the rocket.
178

Divisional Charts in Astrology
 Greek only uses 1 chart.
 Eastern astrology uses around 12 to 17
different chart for a detail analysis of your life.
As you know in Greek they only use 1 chart, which just
shows the positions of the planet and what they are doing in
your life. But, can just 1 chart and position of planets tell the
whole story? Yes, perhaps, but ask yourself. Does a tree only
have one branch, no, it has many.
In Eastern astrology you have many charts. Let's say you
ask an astrologer, Greek or eastern doesn’t matter, you ask
them, what career should I chose?
The Greek and eastern astrologer, if they are good, will
not just look at the 10th house, or 10th lord, or the planet
sitting in the 10th house to tell you the career. There are
179

bunch of combinations and things an astrologer needs to
look just in your main birth chart to give you a true story.
He or she will not just look at your 10th house, or its
placement, but they will look the aspect of the planets on
10th house, or planets aspecting the tenth lord in your chart.
“LORD” means, planets that control its own Zodiac sign,
like Sun is the lord of Leo.
Let's say a good astrologer even does all that I mentioned
above, but, will he tell you about your exact career? NO. He
may tell you, “You should go into artistic field.” The
problem here is which artistic field: Acting, panting, writing,
directing, fashion design? This question will be answered in
a divisional chart.
I went to 8 different astrologers and they all said I should
be in the medical field or hotel management, and I was in
neither, except two astrologers answered me correctly. Who
advised me I should go into writing and stick to artistic field
such as staying behind the camera?
How did they tell me that when they, too, were looking
at the same charts as others? Well, they were not just looking
at 1 chart; they were looking at other branches, meaning
other charts.
Like I said earlier, each aspect of your life, which could
be career, marriage, business, love, children, travel or
education, has its own separate chart, made out from your
main birth chart. These, in Vedic astrology are called
divisional charts, or (D charts, that will go from 1 through
16). You’ll hear this lot on internet, D-1 chart, D-9 chart, D-10
chart on Vedic astrology websites and discussion board.
180

These are your divisional chart holding a deeper look into
each aspect of life.
CHARTS:
D-1 (Rashi chart) chart is your main birth chart.
D-2 (Hora) chart tells your wealth.
D-3 (Drekkana) talks about your co-born.
D-7 (Saptmasha) tells about your general happiness in
life and children.
D-9 (Navamsa) chart, talks about your marriage, and
married life and overall fortune in life with your spouse. D9
is also used to see your career as well.
D-10 (Dasamsa) chart talks about the exact career you
should be in and the kind of authority and status you'll have
in the world. Most books read astrologers say D-9 is your
career chart, because it shows your fortune in the future, but
they are wrong. D-9 is strictly for marriage, and how your
marriage life will be, that’s it! You want to know about
career, go to D-10.
When you read your D9 chart, read it exactly the same
way you would read your regular birth chart, but what D9
shows is the 'What'. If your main birth chart is the 'Who',
then D9 chart is the 'What'. What do you I mean by this?
well, your main birth chart shows who you are according to
the planets in your main birth chart, but D9 'Navamasa'
chart shows what you will do with those placement of that
chart.
181

Since D9 also shows marriage, you can see the 'When'
and 'Who'. Main birth chart shows when you will get
married, while the D9 chart shows 'Who' will you get
married? what type of a girl or boy you will marry is seen
from the D9 chart.
All these can be Googled, and you’ll get a good picture of
what I am talking about.
What D-10 does, it takes your 10th house condition in
your main chart, and makes a detailed planetary alignment
in a new chart, talking just about your career. In D-10 chart,
you’ll then only look towards the 10th house and 10th house
lord, or planets in the 10th house and nothing else.
The 16 divisional charts are an interior of your 12 houses.
It’s like surgically opening each houses, and examining it
from within, and seeing what’s exactly happening there, and
how everything is functioning. Even though there are 16
divisional charts, most well versed Vedic astrologers will
use 2-3 divisional charts only.
Main charts are:
NAVMASHA- marriage
DASAMASA- career
SAPTAMNSA- children and happiness after marriage
TRIMAMSA- misfortune, accidents and diseases
SASTHIAMSA- finer divisional of one's personality and
goals, used especially in birth of twins.
182

How to study Navmasha chart? (D9)
i.e.
If in your main birth chart you have a debilitated Sun in
the 2nd house, but in Navmasha chart it is exalted in the
10th house; this mean that when Sun gets activated in your
birth chart during its dasha or antar dasha, it will give less
negative results regarding your ego, reputation, dealing
with government, father and any kind of job related issue
since Sun is the karaka of 10th house (career) in D1 chart.
This is due to the fact when you opened the heart of Sun,
which is the D9 chart, it showed a very strong heart even
though Sun had weak appearance in the main birth chart
(D1). This shows that even though this person may have low
self-esteem, low confidence and bad relationship with father
and family early on in life, over time, especially in their mid-
life, they will over come all these insecurities brought upon
by weak Sun.
Another thing to notice about D9 chart is the karaka of
D9 chart, which is Venus. In the main birth chart Sun
becomes the atmakaraka of D1 chart. In D9 Venus becomes
the atmakaraka since Venus signifies marriage, wife, love,
relationship and wealth. When looking at the D9 chart
always look at the position of Venus because this will shows
the true foundation of Navmasha chart on which its house is
built upon, even for women Venus should be judged in D9
chart since it is the main karaka of marriage, love &
relationship.

Since D9 is about looking at the 9th house main birth
chart with a microscope, then 9th house of D9 chart also
becomes important. A well placed 9th house along with
Venus helps other factors of the D9 chart.
Any planet in Kendra house of D9 in good condition
becomes a very strong planet. Its effect and result will be
seen during its dasha and antar dasha period. Think of the
condition of planets in D9 chart as the internal body of each
planet or the condition of their heart. A person can look
really good from outside, but if they have a weak heart, bad
digestive track, lungs and liver then the person is truly
weak. There is a great saying, "Don't judge a book by its
cover". This quote can be applied to the study of divisional
chart along side the main birth chart. You can't judge the
main birth chart without looking at the 'Varga' aka
'Divisional charts'.
Only the dasha and antar dasha planet are truly active in
your chart and those are the only planets that should be
really looked upon for current predictions.
This will make more sense in 'How to judge a dasha'
chapter of this book.
How to study Dasamsa chart? (D10)
The same principle of studying D9 chart are applied in
D10 (Dasamsa chart) but in D10 chart the first we do is look
at the condition of 10th house, it's depositor and the planet
sitting in the 10th house. Then we look at the placement of
the ascendant lord of D10 chart. The rule of thumb is when
184

planets are well placed in kendra houses 1, 4, 7 and 10th
house from the ascendant, they give high status, promotion
and fame in their dasha and antar dasha. Planets in kendra
of D10 chart will become the main locomotive of your career
and life. Most of the people who were struggling in life with
lack of promotion and authority at their work palace were
lacking planets in kendra in D10 chart. But planets in trikona
houses aka 1st, 5th and 9th house are the secondary houses
which give strength, However, D10 chart only adds strength
to the D1 chart, but if D1 chart is strong then weakness in
divisional chart will give somewhat struggle in life. But well
placed planets in D9 and D10 chart give easier opportunities
to climb the ladder of success. A weak D1 chart along with
strong divisional chart shows that the person will go
through lot of struggle in their early life but will eventually
over come all the shortcomings.
If you don't have well placed planets in D9 or D10 chart
then it only says you'll have to work a bit harder to achieve
your goals.
One big mistake most astrologer make is that they do not
count aspects, conjunctions and yogas being formed in
divisional chart, which is wrong. Each divisional chart has
aspects, conjunctions and yoga which give a clear picture of
the events happening in a certain dasha.
i.e.
If Jupiter aspects the 1, 5th, 8th and 9th place form itself
in D1 chart then in other divisional chart it has the same
aspects.
185

If Jupiter and Rahu conjunction creates 'Guru Chandal
Yoga' in D1, the main birth chart, then their conjunction in
other divisional chart also creates the same yoga.
In conclusion, divisional charts are the foundation of
your birth chart, but, if you do not know the exactly time of
your birth then looking at divisional charts may not be such
a good idea because divisional charts change every 5-15
minutes. Even though the planets may not change signs in a
divisional chart so often, but their house position will
change.

This simply means that a single planet has two malefic planets on
both sides.

Good Planets vs. Bad Planets (FB and FM)
What makes a certain planet give good or bad result in one's
horoscope? For this you must first learn about Functional Benefic
and Functional Malefic planets for every ascendant.
Each ascendant has a set of planets that actually become adverse
for the native even if they are benefic.
i.e.
Aquarius ascendant: Moon and Mercury become functional
malefic planets, that means even though they are naturally benefic,
they will still cause hardship if they are in conjunction or in aspect
to the benefic planets in the horoscope or during their mahadasha
and antar dasha time period.
Functional malefic are any planet whose moolatrikona sign is in
6th, 8th or 12th house.
Functional benefic planets are those who rule the trikona house for
the ascendant and do not have their moolatrikona sign in 6th, 8th
or 12th house; some kendra lords if they are friendly to the
ascendant also become functional benefic.
Let's find out what are the FM and FB of all ascendants.
Functional Benefic:
Aries- Mars, Sun, Jupiter and Moon
Taurus- Venus, Saturn and Mercury
Gemini- Mercury, Venus and Saturn
Cancer- Moon and Mars
Leo- Sun, Mars and Jupiter
Virgo- Mercury and Venus
Libra- Venus and Saturn
Scorpio- Mars, Jupiter, Moon and Sun
197

Sagittarius- Jupiter, Mars, Mercury and Sun
Capricorn- Venus, Saturn, Mercury
Aquarius- Saturn and Venus
Pisces- Jupiter, Moon and Mars
Functional Malefic:
Aries- Mercury, Rahu, Ketu
Taurus- Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Rahu, Ketu
Gemini- Rahu and Ketu
Cancer- Jupiter, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu
Leo- Moon, Rahu, Ketu
Virgo- Sun, Mars, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu
Libra- Mercury, Rahu, Ketu
Scorpio- Venus, Rahu, Ketu
Sagittarius- Moon, Rahu, Ketu
Capricorn- Sun, Jupiter, Rahu, Ketu
Aquarius- Moon, Mercury, Rahu, Ketu
Pisces- Sun, Saturn, Venus, Rahu, Ketu
Rest of the planets that are not in functional benefic or malefic
table are considered functional neutral, they give results according
to their dignity as in house and sign placement.
Also for Taurus and Scorpio ascendant Venus and Mars are not
really considered functional malefic even though their moolatrikon
sign is placed in the 6th house. Ascendant lord is always the most
benefic planet; for these two ascendants their lords become
functional natural, giving results based on their sign placement and
house placement.
Now that you know the functional malefic and benefic, you'll be
able to understand why certain planets become strong and weak
due to conjunction and aspect.

Just because a planet is in the other house doesn't mean it's not
influencing a planet in the next house. If the degree of two planets
are less than 5 degree even though they are in two different houses,
it is still considered as a 'virtual conjunction'. Meaning, they are
not in actual conjunction but they are close enough to be
considered in conjunction in 'Virtual' terms. Virtual simply means
that something is there but it can not be measured or be seen,
however it's influencing something and creating a reaction. This is
simple cosmology for you. In cosmology you study electrons,
protons and neutrons. Have you ever wondered why is that
electron knows that it has a negative charge and why a proton
knows it has a positive charge? this is due to the virtual particles

What is Atmakaraka?
It simply means a soul significator, meaning, planet that
holds the lesson that souls is here to learn.
Atma= soul
Karka= significator
Sun is the original significator of the soul; this is why it's the
original Atmakaraka. But, there is something called 'Chara'
Atmakaraka.
Chara= moveable. The remaining seven planets like
Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter and Saturn are considered
Chara Atmakaraka. Yes, even Rahu is a part of this table
because Rahu brings past life lessons into this life just like
other planets. Rahu is unfulfilled desire that needs to be
fulfilled in this life. Most did not consider Rahu as chara
Atmakaraka but now after many evaluations and test
results, Rahu is considered a part of the cycle at least in my
experience with chart readings. After doing my own studies,
many people performed exactly the opposite of what Rahu
was here to teach them as Rahu held the highest degree.
If someone was to ask you, what is your Atmakaraka in
your birth chart, you will not say Sun, unless Sun is under
special circumstances and is also your chara atmakaraka.
Why? Let me show you.
Planet with the highest degree is considered as 'chara'
Atmakaraka. Each planet can be between 0-30 degrees, the
225

planet that holds the highest degree amongst all the planets
becomes the chara Atmakaraka.
This way, Sun for someone can become not just Atmakaraka
but also the chara Atmakaraka.
i.e.
SUN - 23.2 Aries
MOON - 15.2 Leo
MERCURY- 6.1 Taurus
VENUS - 23.1 - Aries
MARS - 19- Scorpio
JUPITER -17.4- Cancer
SATURN -18.2- Gemini
RAHU/KETU -13.3- Leo/Aquarius
By looking at the table above, you can clearly see that Sun
holds the highest degrees at 23.2. Sun becomes 'chara'
Atmakaraka.

backwards? Simple subtract Rahu's degrees from 30 and
you'll have your answer. Also remember, Rahu and Ketu are
always at exact same degrees, one is never ahead or before
the other, this is why Rahu is only considered in this
calculation and as Atmakaraka, because of Rahu's desires,
Ketu's journey is fulfilled and Ketu has no desires and goals.

looking at the chara Atmakaraka.

Hora Chart for Wealth: Wealth secrets
263

Hora means hour, and is quite an important part of Vedic
astrology. When you get your full horoscope by an
astrologer, you'll see something called a Hora Chart. This
chart determines which areas of your life need work, and
which areas of your life give you the best result without
asking.
All people are either born in the Hora of the Sun or Hora of
the Moon. You need to determine which Hora you were
born into. Hora of the Sun is about daytime, and Hora of the
moon is about night time. Planets that are strong in Hora of
the Sun are the Sun, Venus, and Jupiter. Planets that are
strong in Hora of the Moon are the Moon, Mars, and Saturn.
Mercury is strong depending upon the time you were born.
If the birth took place during sunrise or sunset, then
Mercury will be strong for both Horas, otherwise it's usually
neutral.
To determine your Hora, look at your ascendant or 1st
House, and look at the degrees of your rising sign. All
horoscopes show the degree table of your ascendant and
planets. People are born between 0-30 degrees; nothing
below and nothing above. The Horas are divided into 2
parts: the first 15 degrees and the second 15 degrees. People
born in the first 15 degrees of the rising sign will be part of
Hora 1, while people born in the second half of the degrees
(from 16-30), will be part of Hora 2.
How do you find determine if you are in the Hora of the Sun
or Moon? If you are born in even rising signs like Taurus,
Cancer, Virgo, Scorpio, Capricorn, and Pisces, then your first
15 degrees will be ruled by the Moon. This means that the
Moon, Mars and Saturn are strong for you. The original
264

houses they rule, meaning for all the even rising signs the
1st, 4th, 8th, 10th, and 11th House will be strong since they
are originally ruled by Mars, Moon and Saturn.
If you were born in odd rising signs like Aries, Gemini, Leo,
Libra, Sagittarius, and Aquarius, and if you were born in the
first 15 degrees of the rising sign, you are ruled by the Hora
of the Sun. If you were born in the second half of the
degrees, then you were born in the Hora of the Moon.
Example 1:
If an Aries ascendant native was born in the Hora of the
Moon, they would run into problems related to planets
which are controlled by the Hora of the Sun like the 2nd,
5th, 7th, 9th and 12th House which are ruled by Sun, Venus,
and Jupiter. One can judge the 3rd and 6th House by seeing
the time of birth, and if the native was born during sunrise
or sunset. Otherwise, these houses will be neutral for the
native.
Example 2:
If the Aries ascendant native was born in the first half of the
degrees, then they were born in the Hora of the Sun. The
problem houses would be ruled by the Hora of the Moon
265

like the 1st, 4th, 8th, 10th, and 11th House, which are houses
ruled by the Moon, Mars and Saturn.
Example 3:
If you are born under the Aries ascendant and Moon Hora,
then the 2nd, 5th, 7th, 9th and 12th House become a source
of struggle and problems, while the 1st, 4th, 8th, 10th, and
11th House are a source of help. If Jupiter, the original lord
of 9th and 12th House, is sitting in the 4th House, it helps
the 9th and 12th House, and is less problematic because the
4th house is good for Hora of the Moon. If this was Hora of
the Sun, and Jupiter was in the 4th house, then problems
would arise.
If you are born at night while in the Hora of the Moon, then
it is icing on a cake because throughout your life you'll have
support of your mother, in-laws, bosses, and social network.
This also applies to being born in the day time while in the
Hora of the Sun. There will be support from family, ability
to save money, have children, and gain a supportive spouse.
There will also be support from one’s father and teachers.
Rahu and Ketu give results according to the same concept.
For the Moon Hora, houses 1, 4, 7, 8, 10 and 11 are good.
Houses 3 and 6 depend upon Mercury. The same goes for
266

the Sun Hora. This is why half the population will get good
results of Rahu in 5th House while other half will get bad
results.
